# Error Handling Checklist: PRSG-008 Layer Planner

**Purpose**: Validate that planner failure modes, warning behavior, exit codes, stdout JSON, and stderr summaries are complete, consistent, and fixture-testable.
**Created**: 2026-06-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Domain**: error-handling · **Audience**: PR reviewer · **Depth**: formal release gate

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are missing required heading failures explicitly mapped to `missing_required_heading`, `invalid_plan`, and exit `1`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014g, Spec §FR-014i, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK002 Are unknown increment references and empty declared increments explicitly mapped to invalid-plan errors rather than warnings or input errors? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014g, Data Model §Increment]
- [ ] CHK003 Are dependency cycles explicitly covered as `dependency_cycle` invalid-plan errors? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014g, Data Model §Increment]
- [ ] CHK004 Are malformed checkbox task-like lines explicitly covered as `malformed_task` invalid-plan errors? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014j, Data Model §Task]
- [ ] CHK005 Are unreadable feature directories and unreadable `tasks.md` files explicitly mapped to `input_error`, severity `error`, and exit `2`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014k, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK006 Are missing file and test references explicitly modeled as warning diagnostics that do not fail an otherwise valid plan? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014h, Spec §SC-005]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is the status-to-exit-code mapping closed and unambiguous for `ok`, `invalid_plan`, and `input_error`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014b, Spec §FR-015, Contract §Exit Codes]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the distinction between malformed task-plan content and unreadable invocation/input surfaces clear enough to prevent exit `1` and exit `2` conflation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-014g, Spec §FR-014k]
- [ ] CHK009 Are invalid-plan diagnostics required to emit structured JSON errors to stdout while stderr remains a concise human-readable summary? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK010 Are warning diagnostics required to share the same structured shape as errors while retaining severity `warning`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014e, Spec §FR-014m, Contract §Diagnostics]
- [ ] CHK011 Is `reference_not_found.details.kind` constrained to distinguish `file` from `test` references without message parsing? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014h, Spec §FR-014l, Contract §Diagnostics]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do the spec, data model, contract, and schema agree that invalid-plan and input-error diagnostic codes use severity `error`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014m, Data Model §Diagnostic, Schema §diagnostic]
- [ ] CHK013 Do the spec, data model, contract, and schema agree that `task_without_references` and `reference_not_found` use severity `warning`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014h, Data Model §Diagnostic, Schema §warningDiagnostic]
- [ ] CHK014 Do the spec, contract, and schema agree on the complete invalid-plan code list? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014g, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.code]
- [ ] CHK015 Do the spec, contract, and schema agree on the complete input-error code list? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014k, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.code]
- [ ] CHK016 Do the spec, contract, and schema agree on closed, per-code `details` payload requirements? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014l, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.details]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can malformed-plan fixtures objectively assert exit `1`, `status=invalid_plan`, non-empty `errors[]`, empty or warning-only `warnings[]` as applicable, and a concise stderr summary? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Can usage/input fixtures objectively assert exit `2`, `status=input_error`, structured JSON error output, and no repository writes? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Can missing-reference fixtures objectively assert exit `0`, `status=ok`, warning diagnostics, and continued plan emission? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK020 Can tests assert stdout/stderr separation without parsing human-readable stderr for machine decisions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-014l]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are primary success, invalid-plan, input-error, and warning-only scenarios all covered by requirements and success criteria? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK022 Are autopilot stop behaviors defined separately for planner exit `1` and exit `2`? [Coverage, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-018a, Spec §FR-018b]
- [ ] CHK023 Is warning propagation into autopilot context defined for exit `0` with warnings? [Coverage, Spec §FR-018c, Plan §Phase 1 Design Decisions]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK024 Are missing `## Dependencies & Execution Order` and missing `### Incremental Delivery` headings both covered under `missing_required_heading`? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014i]
- [ ] CHK025 Are duplicate increment IDs and duplicate task IDs both covered with source locations for first and duplicate occurrences? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014g, Spec §FR-014l]
- [ ] CHK026 Are contradictory order, unknown increment, and dependency-cycle failures distinguishable by code and details payload? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014g, Spec §FR-014l]
- [ ] CHK027 Are invocation arity, missing feature directory, unreadable feature directory, missing `tasks.md`, and unreadable `tasks.md` all covered as input errors? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014k]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK028 Does the plan name the existing shell-test harness as the verification surface for exit-code and stream behavior? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK029 Are warning semantics aligned with the planner-only non-goal so missing references do not trigger PRSG-009 branch or restack behavior in PRSG-008? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-019]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: exit-code mapping for missing headings, invalid references, cycles, unreadable inputs, and malformed tasks; structured JSON errors to stdout; concise stderr summaries; missing file/test references as warnings.
- Defaults applied from checklist workflow: reviewer audience, formal release-gate depth.
- Result: no error-handling gaps were identified. Existing PRSG-008 artifacts already specify the requested behaviors.
